channel.title: GenAI Systems Lab — Ground Truth
channel.description: Deep technical posts on production AI systems: RAG, agents, LLMOps, fine-tuning, and more.

item.title: What Is a Transformer? Self-Attention Explained Without the Math
item.description: Why every LLM is built on the same core idea — and what attention actually computes, step by step.

item.title: Self-Attention: From Dot Products to What the Model Focuses On
item.description: Query, Key, Value matrices explained. Why multi-head attention sees different things at once, and what that means for long-range dependencies.

item.title: Tokenization: Why
item.description: BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece — what tokenizers do, why it matters for prompting, and how token counts drive your inference bill.

item.title: Embeddings Explained: How Text Becomes Geometry
item.description: What embedding vectors represent, why semantic similarity works, and how this underpins every RAG system and search product.

item.title: Temperature, Top-P, Top-K: How LLMs Actually Choose the Next Word
item.description: Greedy, beam search, nucleus sampling — what each one does, when randomness helps, and why temperature 0 isn

item.title: The Context Window: What Goes In, What Gets Dropped, and Why It Matters
item.description: How LLMs process long inputs, what context overflow looks like in production, and strategies to stay within the window without losing what matters.

item.title: Prompt Engineering & Token Economics
item.description: How prompt structure affects quality, why few-shot beats zero-shot in most cases, and how to calculate real inference cost before you ship.

item.title: How RAG Actually Works — And Why It
item.description: The full retrieval-augmented generation pipeline: chunking → embedding → retrieval → reranking → generation. Where each step silently fails.

item.title: Chunking Strategies for RAG: Fixed, Semantic, and Hierarchical
item.description: Why chunk size is one of the most impactful RAG config decisions. Fixed-size vs. sentence vs. semantic chunking, with real retrieval quality differences.

item.title: RAG Architectures: Naive, Advanced, Modular, and Agentic
item.description: How RAG has evolved from a simple retrieve-and-read loop to routing, query rewriting, self-RAG, corrective RAG, and full agentic retrieval.

item.title: Reranking: Why Top-K Retrieval Isn
item.description: How cross-encoders and rerankers improve precision after initial retrieval, when the latency cost is worth it, and how to evaluate reranker quality.

item.title: Vector Databases Compared: Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, pgvector
item.description: Latency, scale, metadata filtering, cost, and managed vs. self-hosted tradeoffs. A decision framework for choosing your vector store.

item.title: Missing Context: When RAG Retrieves the Right Chunk but Answers the Wrong Question
item.description: Why high similarity score doesn

item.title: Ambiguous Queries: Why RAG Struggles When the Question Has Two Meanings
item.description: Multi-intent queries, under-specified questions, and how your retriever picks the wrong meaning — and confidently answers it.

item.title: Hybrid Search: Combining BM25 and Vector Retrieval
item.description: Why pure semantic search misses exact matches, and pure keyword search misses meaning. How hybrid search with RRF fusion beats both.

item.title: The ReAct Pattern: How LLM Agents Reason and Act
item.description: Thought → Action → Observation loops explained. How ReAct enables tool use, where it breaks, and how to trace what your agent actually did.

item.title: Tool Use Design for AI Agents: Contracts, Consequences, and MCP
item.description: How to design tools an agent won

item.title: 6 Types of Memory in AI Agents (And When to Use Each)
item.description: In-context, episodic, semantic, procedural, working, and external memory — what each stores, how it

item.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration: Supervisor, Pipeline, and Mesh Patterns
item.description: How to break a complex task across multiple agents. Supervisor vs. pipeline vs. mesh patterns, inter-agent communication, and failure budgets.

item.title: Planning Patterns for AI Agents: ToT, GoT, and LATS
item.description: How agents plan ahead: Tree of Thought, Graph of Thought, LATS, and reflection loops. When complex planning beats straight ReAct.

item.title: Tracing Agent Loops: How to Debug Step-by-Step Execution
item.description: What a step trace reveals, how to spot loops, wrong tool calls, and hallucinated observations — and how to use the Agent Loop Simulator to reproduce failures.

item.title: How AI Agents Fail in Production: A Full Taxonomy
item.description: Tool misuse, infinite loops, hallucinated tool calls, context bleed, approval fatigue, compounding reliability failures — with worked examples from the lab.

item.title: How to Evaluate LLM Systems: RAGAS, G-Eval, and Custom Grading
item.description: What groundedness, faithfulness, and citation accuracy actually measure — and how to build an eval pipeline that catches real failures before users do.

item.title: Hallucination Detection: Why It
item.description: Factual vs. faithfulness vs. citation hallucinations. NLI-based detection, self-consistency, and retrieval grounding — tested against real examples.

item.title: Building an Eval Pipeline That Actually Catches Production Failures
item.description: Why unit tests aren

item.title: A/B Testing LLM Systems: Statistical Significance and Evaluation Metrics
item.description: How to run controlled experiments on LLM outputs, which metrics to use (win-rate, NDCG, preference), and how to avoid common A/B traps.

item.title: LLMOps: What Production AI Actually Needs That Tutorials Skip
item.description: Observability, prompt versioning, latency budgets, cost tracking, model routers, A/B testing, and rollback strategies. The full production checklist.

item.title: Fine-Tuning vs. RAG vs. Prompt Engineering: When to Use What
item.description: The decision framework every AI engineer needs. Cost, latency, data requirements, update frequency, and failure modes for each approach.

item.title: Model Routing: How to Send the Right Query to the Right Model
item.description: Complexity-based routing, cost-based routing, and capability routing. How model routers cut inference cost by 40-70% without hurting quality.

item.title: LLM Inference Optimisation: Batching, Quantisation, and Speculative Decoding
item.description: How to reduce latency and cost at inference time without retraining. INT8/INT4 quantisation, continuous batching, speculative decoding explained.

item.title: Prompt Caching and Token Cost Optimisation at Scale
item.description: How prompt caching works, when it pays off, and how to reduce inference cost by 60-80% on repetitive system prompt workloads.

item.title: LLM Observability: What to Log, Trace, and Alert On
item.description: Prompt/response logging, latency tracing, cost tracking, quality signals, and alert thresholds. What a production-grade LLM monitoring stack looks like.

item.title: ML CI/CD: Testing, Versioning, and Deploying LLM Pipelines
item.description: How to adapt software CI/CD for ML: prompt versioning, eval regression gates, canary deploys, and rollback for model updates.

item.title: Context Compaction: Managing Long Conversations Without Losing the Thread
item.description: Why conversation history grows until it breaks. Sliding window, summarisation-based, and hierarchical compaction strategies for production agents.

item.title: Cost vs. Latency Tradeoffs in LLM Systems: How to Budget Both
item.description: TTFT, tokens-per-second, and end-to-end latency explained. How to set SLAs, model latency against user tolerance, and build a cost/latency budget.

item.title: Shadow Mode Testing: How to Compare Models Before You Switch
item.description: Run a new model in shadow mode alongside your live model, compare outputs without user exposure, and make data-driven upgrade decisions.

item.title: Model Strategy: When to Use GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, or an Open Model
item.description: The model selection decision — capability, cost, latency, data privacy, and fine-tunability. How to build a model strategy that holds up as models evolve.

item.title: Guardrails for LLMs: Input/Output Filtering in Production
item.description: How guardrail pipelines work — input classifiers, output validators, topic filters, PII redaction, and toxicity detection. What fails at scale.

item.title: Prompt Injection in Production: Attacks, Defenses, and What Doesn
item.description: Direct and indirect injection, jailbreaks, data exfiltration via prompt, and why input sanitisation alone isn

item.title: Red Teaming LLMs: A Structured Methodology
item.description: How to systematically find failure modes before attackers do. Adversarial prompting, boundary testing, multi-turn attacks, and red team documentation.

item.title: Bias in LLM Outputs: Sources, Types, and What You Can Detect
item.description: Training data bias, demographic representation, positional bias in RAG, and confirmation bias in reasoning. How to surface and measure these in your system.

item.title: Privacy and Compliance for LLM Systems
item.description: PII in prompts, data residency, model training on user data, GDPR/CCPA implications, and how to build a compliance architecture for production LLMs.

item.title: A Framework for AI System Design Interviews (Staff+ Level)
item.description: 6-axis characterisation, architecture shape selection, reliability budgets, and how to structure a 45-minute system design answer.

item.title: Designing a Production RAG System: Full Architecture Walkthrough
item.description: Document ingestion pipeline, retrieval layer, reranker, answer policy, eval loop, and monitoring — all the decisions you need to make before you ship.

item.title: Designing an Agent System for Production: State, Tools, and Failure Handling
item.description: How to design an agent that doesn

item.title: Building AI at India Scale: Latency, Language, and Cost Constraints
item.description: What changes when you build for 500ms mobile latency, 22 official languages, and $0.001/query cost targets. Architecture decisions for India-scale AI.

item.title: Structured Outputs from LLMs: JSON Mode, Function Calling, and Tool Use
item.description: How to make LLMs output reliable, parseable data. JSON mode, OpenAI function calling, Pydantic validation, and when structured outputs break.

item.title: Case Study: How Stale Documents Made a Compliance Chatbot Confidently Wrong
item.description: Two policy versions in the corpus, top_k=1, no freshness filter. The chatbot answered with 3-year-old data. How to reproduce and fix this exact failure.

item.title: Multi-Hop Reasoning Failures: When Your RAG System Can
item.description: Why single-step retrieval fails on questions that require chaining two or three facts. How multi-hop and three-hop failures look, and how to architect around them.

item.title: Context Overflow: What Happens When Your RAG Pipeline Runs Out of Space
item.description: How context overflow silently truncates the most relevant content, why it

item.title: The Incident Room: How to Respond to LLM Production Failures
item.description: A playbook for LLM incidents — how to triage, isolate, mitigate, and do a post-mortem. What

item.title: When Your LLM Is Too Slow: Diagnosing and Fixing Latency Regressions
item.description: How to identify whether latency is in TTFT, TPS, retrieval, or network. A step-by-step latency triage guide with the Latency Planner tool.

item.title: Context Bleed: When One User
item.description: The subtle session isolation failure that affects multi-tenant LLM apps. How system prompt fragments, conversation history, and cached context leak across users — and what it costs you.

item.title: Tool Loop: When Your Agent Can
item.description: The runaway agent failure that burns tokens, triggers rate limits, and produces no output. How tool loops form, how to detect them in traces, and the circuit-breaker patterns that stop them.

item.title: Silent Hallucinations: The Confident Wrong Answer That Nobody Catches
item.description: Why the most dangerous LLM failure isn

item.title: Cost Explosion: How a Single Prompt Change Multiplied Our API Bill by 40×
item.description: A production post-mortem: how an innocent prompt refactor removed a compression step, what the token math looked like in real time, and the guardrails that should have caught it.

item.title: Retrieval Poisoning: How Bad Documents Corrupt Your Entire RAG Pipeline
item.description: What happens when stale, contradictory, or adversarially crafted documents enter your vector store. How contamination propagates, why it

item.title: Eval Gaming: When Your Model Passes Tests but Fails Users
item.description: The silent regression that looks like progress. How LLMs learn to game specific benchmarks and human preference labels, why your held-out test set stops being held-out, and what floor-preserving evals look like.

item.title: Prompt Injection Bypasses: When Your Safety Instructions Don
item.description: The injection patterns that defeat system prompts in production — indirect injection via retrieved documents, role-play escapes, encoding tricks. What defence-in-depth looks like for real apps.

item.title: Cold Start Latency: Why Your Serverless LLM Endpoint Is Spiking
item.description: Container cold starts, model loading, connection pool exhaustion — the latency cliff that hits low-traffic LLM endpoints. Diagnosis, warm-up strategies, and when to give up on serverless.

item.title: Schema Drift: When Your Structured Output Parser Silently Breaks
item.description: How adding one optional field to a function-call schema breaks 12% of responses in ways your monitoring misses. The versioning discipline, canary rollout, and output validation layer that prevent it.

item.title: Cascade Failure: When an LLM Mistake Breaks the Entire Downstream Pipeline
item.description: How a single hallucinated field value propagates through a multi-step agent pipeline, what the blast radius looks like in production, and the isolation checkpoints that contain it.

item.title: Confidence Calibration: Why
item.description: Why LLM verbalized confidence (

item.title: Reranker Inversion: When Your Reranker Makes Retrieval Worse
item.description: The counterintuitive failure where adding a reranker reduces end-to-end RAG quality. Why cross-encoder rerankers underperform on domain-specific queries, and how to measure before you deploy.

item.title: Writing PRDs for AI Features: A Framework for Product Managers
item.description: What makes AI PRDs different — uncertainty ranges, fallback behaviour, eval criteria, human-in-the-loop decisions, and what

item.title: AI-or-Not? A Decision Framework for Product Managers
item.description: When AI is the right solution and when it

item.title: AI Roadmap Prioritisation: How to Decide What to Build Next
item.description: Impact/feasibility for AI — why standard prioritisation frameworks break. How to score AI initiatives accounting for data readiness, model risk, and iteration speed.

item.title: Explaining AI to Non-Technical Stakeholders: Frameworks That Work
item.description: How to explain RAG, agents, hallucination risk, and eval gaps to execs, legal, and ops teams — without losing them or dumbing it down.

item.title: The AI Launch Checklist: What to Verify Before Going Live
item.description: Eval gate, fallback behaviour, latency SLA, cost guardrails, safety review, legal sign-off, monitoring setup — the full pre-launch checklist for AI features.

item.title: How to Read a Model Card: What PMs and Engineers Actually Need to Know
item.description: What model cards tell you about training data, benchmarks, limitations, and bias — and how to use them to make informed model selection decisions.

item.title: LLM Interview Question Patterns: What Senior Engineers Actually Ask
item.description: The 10 question categories, common traps, and how to structure 4-layer answers. From

item.title: 25 RAG Interview Questions With Model Answers
item.description: Covering retrieval, chunking, reranking, evaluation, failure modes, and system design — with strong answers and the traps interviewers use to filter candidates.

item.title: How to Ace an AI Case Interview: Structure, Signals, and What Kills Candidates
item.description: How top companies structure AI case interviews, what signals they

item.title: The AI Vocabulary Cheat Sheet: 80 Terms You Need to Know Cold
item.description: From attention to zero-shot — the 80 terms that come up in AI interviews, product reviews, and engineering discussions. Definitions plus context for each.

item.title: How to Crush AI Take-Home Challenges: What Evaluators Actually Look For
item.description: What separates strong from weak take-homes — problem framing, eval design, failure analysis, and documentation. A checklist before you submit.

item.title: AI Benchmarks Explained: What MMLU, HumanEval, HELM, and LMSYS Actually Measure
item.description: What each benchmark tests, its known weaknesses, and how to use benchmark results to make real hiring and model selection decisions without being misled.

item.title: Context Tetris: Why What You Put in the Prompt Matters as Much as the Model
item.description: How to think about context window real estate — system prompt, examples, retrieved chunks, history, and query. Optimising the slot machine that is your prompt.

item.title: How Claude Works: Constitutional AI, 200K Context, and What Makes It Different
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: How ChatGPT Works: GPT-4o, RLHF, and the o1 Reasoning Models
item.description: From the base GPT model to RLHF fine-tuning to GPT-4o

item.title: How Gemini Works: 1M Context, Native Multimodality, and Google
item.description: Gemini

item.title: Llama 3 and the Open-Source Model Ecosystem: What You Can Build
item.description: Meta

item.title: Mistral, Cohere, and the Frontier Beyond OpenAI/Anthropic
item.description: Mistral

item.title: Reading Model Benchmarks Without Being Misled
item.description: MMLU, HumanEval, LMSYS Chatbot Arena, HELM, SWE-bench — what each measures, its known flaws, and how to pick a model based on your actual use case, not marketing.

item.title: How Top Tech Companies Are Rebuilding Their Products Around AI
item.description: Google

item.title: AI in Fintech: Fraud Detection, Underwriting, and Compliance Automation
item.description: How banks and fintechs are deploying LLMs and ML for real-time fraud detection, credit decisioning, document processing, and regulatory reporting.

item.title: AI in Healthcare: Clinical NLP, Medical Coding, and the Hallucination Problem
item.description: Where AI is being deployed in clinical workflows, why hallucination is an existential risk in medical AI, and the regulatory landscape for health LLMs.

item.title: AI Features in Enterprise SaaS: What
item.description: The patterns that deliver real enterprise value (copilots, intelligent search, workflow automation) vs. AI features shipped for press release purposes.

item.title: The Solo Operator: How AI Lets One Person Run a Business That Used to Need a Team
item.description: AI as a force multiplier for individuals — the real workflows (content, support, code, research, outreach) where solo operators are replacing teams.

item.title: What Is an AI Engineer? Role, Skills, and How It Differs from ML Engineer
item.description: The new AI Engineer role — what companies actually want, the technical stack (RAG, agents, evals, LLMOps), and how it differs from ML Engineering and Data Science.

item.title: What Does an ML Engineer Actually Do in 2025?
item.description: The evolving ML Engineer role post-LLM revolution — what

item.title: The AI Product Manager: What
item.description: How AI PM differs from traditional PM — working with probabilistic systems, writing AI-specific PRDs, speaking the language of evals, and hiring the right team.

item.title: AI/ML Salary Guide 2025: Ranges, Levers, and How to Negotiate
item.description: Real comp data for AI Engineer, ML Engineer, Research Scientist, and AI PM across US, UK, EU, and India — plus the specific skills and signals that move your number.

item.title: Breaking Into AI: The Fastest Path from Software Engineer to AI Engineer
item.description: The exact learning path, project types, and portfolio signals that get backend/frontend engineers hired as AI engineers — in 3-6 months, not 3 years.

item.title: The Minimum Tech Stack for Every AI Role, Level, and Company Tier
item.description: Exactly what tools, frameworks, and skills are expected from AI Engineers, ML Engineers, AI PMs, FDEs, and MLOps engineers — at junior, mid, senior, and staff level.

item.title: Attention Is All You Need: The Paper That Built the AI Era
item.description: The 2017 Transformer paper that replaced RNNs, enabled parallel training, and became the foundation every LLM is built on. What it actually proposed and why it worked.

item.title: InstructGPT: How RLHF Turned a Language Model Into an Assistant
item.description: The 2022 OpenAI paper that introduced RLHF to mainstream AI. How SFT + reward model + PPO produced a model that followed instructions — and why it changed everything.

item.title: Constitutional AI: How Anthropic Trains Claude to Be Helpful and Harmless
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: Chinchilla: The Scaling Laws Paper That Made Bigger Models Smaller
item.description: DeepMind

item.title: Chain-of-Thought: The Prompting Technique That Unlocked LLM Reasoning
item.description: Google

item.title: The Original RAG Paper: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Explained
item.description: Facebook AI

item.title: ReAct: The Paper That Gave LLMs the Ability to Reason and Act Together
item.description: Princeton/Google

item.title: Toolformer: How Language Models Learned to Use APIs on Their Own
item.description: Meta AI

item.title: LIMA: Why 1,000 Examples Beat Millions for Instruction Tuning
item.description: Meta AI

item.title: The GPT-4 Technical Report: What OpenAI Told Us (and What They Didn
item.description: The most-read model paper that reveals almost nothing about architecture or training. What the report actually contains — benchmark analysis, safety evaluations, system card — and how to read it.

item.title: Mixtral and Mixture of Experts: Activating 13B Parameters to Deliver 70B Quality
item.description: Mistral AI

item.title: CLIP: The Paper That Made Vision and Language Speak the Same Language
item.description: OpenAI

item.title: LoRA: How Low-Rank Adaptation Made Fine-Tuning Accessible to Everyone
item.description: Microsoft

item.title: DPO: Direct Preference Optimization — Alignment Without a Reward Model
item.description: Stanford

item.title: FlashAttention: The Algorithmic Fix That Made Long Context Actually Work
item.description: Stanford

item.title: Fine-Tuning Fundamentals: What It Is, When to Use It, and When Not To
item.description: The mental model every engineer needs before touching fine-tuning. What fine-tuning actually changes in a model, the three real use cases where it beats prompting, and the five situations where it

item.title: Full Fine-Tuning vs. PEFT vs. Prompting: The Decision Framework
item.description: A practical decision tree for choosing between full fine-tuning, LoRA/QLoRA, and prompt engineering — based on data size, latency requirements, update frequency, and budget.

item.title: LoRA in Practice: A Step-by-Step Production Fine-Tuning Guide
item.description: From dataset prep to adapter merge to deployment. The exact config choices that matter — rank, alpha, target modules, learning rate, epochs — with what goes wrong at each step.

item.title: QLoRA: Fine-Tuning 70B Models on a Single GPU
item.description: How 4-bit quantisation + LoRA adapters make large model fine-tuning accessible. NF4 quantisation explained, double quantisation, bfloat16 adapters, and what you lose vs. full LoRA.

item.title: PEFT Methods Compared: LoRA, Prefix Tuning, Prompt Tuning, and Adapters
item.description: The four main parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches — what each trains, when each wins, and why LoRA dominates most production use cases despite not being the oldest method.

item.title: Building Instruction Tuning Datasets: Quality Over Everything
item.description: The data decisions that determine whether your fine-tuned model improves or degrades. Format design, diversity requirements, quality filtering, synthetic data generation, and the LIMA principle in practice.

item.title: DPO in Practice: Building Preference Datasets and Running Alignment Training
item.description: Beyond the theory — how to actually collect preference data, format (prompt, chosen, rejected) triplets, run DPO training with PEFT, and evaluate the result against your baseline.

item.title: Evaluating Fine-Tuned Models: How to Know If You Actually Improved Anything
item.description: Why perplexity is the wrong metric, how to build domain-specific eval sets, what regression testing looks like for fine-tuned models, and how to catch capability degradation.

item.title: Catastrophic Forgetting: Why Fine-Tuning Breaks General Capability
item.description: What catastrophic forgetting actually looks like in fine-tuned LLMs, why it

item.title: Continued Pretraining: When Fine-Tuning Isn
item.description: When domain adaptation requires continued pretraining on unlabelled text rather than supervised fine-tuning. Medical, legal, and code domains — what it takes and when it

item.title: From Fine-Tuned Model to Production: Serving, Versioning, and Monitoring
item.description: The gap between a fine-tuned checkpoint and a production deployment. Adapter merging, quantisation for serving, model versioning strategy, and the monitoring signals that catch regressions.

item.title: Process Reward Models: How o1 and o3 Learn to Think Step by Step
item.description: Why outcome-based RLHF fails on multi-step reasoning and how process reward models — rewarding each step rather than the final answer — produce the reasoning models behind o1, o3, and DeepSeek-R1.

item.title: CLIP: How Contrastive Vision-Language Pretraining Works
item.description: OpenAI

item.title: Vision Transformers (ViT): How Images Become Tokens
item.description: The architecture that replaced CNNs for vision tasks. How patch embeddings work, why transformers scale better than convolutions, and what the attention mechanism sees in an image.

item.title: How Multimodal LLMs Work: LLaVA, GPT-4V, and Gemini Architecture
item.description: How you connect a vision encoder to a language model — the projection layer, visual tokens, and instruction tuning approach behind LLaVA. How GPT-4V and Gemini differ architecturally, and what

item.title: Multimodal RAG: Retrieval Over Images, Tables, and Mixed Documents
item.description: RAG breaks when your corpus has PDFs with charts, scanned documents, or product images. The three architectures for handling mixed-modal retrieval: late fusion, early fusion, and cross-modal reranking — with when each fails.

item.title: Diffusion Models Explained: How Image Generation Actually Works
item.description: What diffusion models actually do: the forward noising process, the reverse denoising network, why U-Net matters, and how text conditioning (CLIP + cross-attention) turns a text prompt into an image. No hand-waving.

item.title: Audio and Speech Models: How Whisper and the Speech Pipeline Works
item.description: From raw audio waveform to text: log-mel spectrograms, the encoder-decoder transformer architecture, why Whisper is so robust to accents and noise, and how to deploy speech-to-text in production at low cost.

item.title: Evaluating Multimodal Systems: Benchmarks, Metrics, and Production Signals
item.description: How do you know if your vision-language model is actually good? MMMU, MMBench, and VQA explained. Hallucination in multimodal models (CHAIR metric). The gap between benchmark scores and production quality.

item.title: Multimodal in Production: Latency, Cost, and Deployment Realities
item.description: Vision tokens are expensive — GPT-4V charges per tile. The real latency and cost profile of multimodal calls vs. text-only, how to pre-process images to cut costs, and the caching strategies that matter for production workloads.

item.title: Image Embeddings and Visual Search: Building Production Image Retrieval
item.description: How CLIP embeddings power reverse image search, product discovery, and content moderation. The full pipeline: embed → index → ANN search → rerank. What breaks at scale and how to fix it.

item.title: How to Evaluate Multi-Step AI Agents
item.description: Why standard LLM evals break for agents. Task completion rate, tool call accuracy, trajectory quality, and building an agent eval harness.

item.title: Prompt Caching: Cut Your LLM Costs by 90% Without Changing Your Prompts
item.description: How prefix caching works in Anthropic Claude and OpenAI APIs, when it applies, how to structure prompts to maximise cache hits, and real cost savings math.

item.title: LLM Security: Prompt Injection, Jailbreaks, and How to Actually Defend Against Them
item.description: The real threat model for production LLM systems. Indirect injection, data exfiltration paths, jailbreak taxonomy, and a practical defence checklist.

item.title: Choosing a Vector Database in 2025: A Decision Framework
item.description: Pinecone vs Weaviate vs Qdrant vs Chroma vs pgvector — when to use each, what the real cost looks like at scale, and how to avoid choosing the wrong one.

item.title: LLM Cost Optimization Playbook: From $10K to $1K Monthly
item.description: A systematic approach to cutting LLM inference costs by 80–90% without degrading quality. Caching, model routing, prompt compression, and batch processing.

item.title: RLHF and DPO: How Language Models Learn Human Preferences
item.description: How reinforcement learning from human feedback works end to end, why DPO replaced it for most teams, and what actually changes in the model during alignment training.

item.title: Constitutional AI: How Anthropic Trains Claude to Be Helpful and Safe
item.description: The CAI technique: using a set of principles to train models to critique and revise their own outputs, reducing the need for human labelers at scale.

item.title: How I
item.description: A concrete walkthrough of the architecture, vendor choices, chunking strategy, and eval harness for an AI search system over company documentation.

item.title: Structured Outputs: JSON Mode, Tool Calling, and Constrained Decoding
item.description: How to reliably get JSON, tables, and typed data from LLMs. JSON mode vs tool calling vs grammar-constrained decoding — what each guarantees and where each breaks.

item.title: Building Memory into AI Agents: LangMem, Mem0, and Custom Patterns
item.description: How to actually implement persistent memory in production agents. Architecture patterns, library comparison, and what breaks at scale.

item.title: Claude 3.5 vs GPT-4o vs Gemini 1.5 Pro: A Practical Comparison
item.description: Not benchmarks — actual task-type tradeoffs. When each model wins, where each fails, and how to choose for your use case.

item.title: How Notion Built AI on a Block-Based Data Model: Architecture Decisions and Lessons
item.description: Notion

item.title: How Perplexity Serves Millions of AI Searches: Search → LLM → Citations at Scale
item.description: Perplexity

item.title: How Cursor Built AI Code Completion: Context, Retrieval, and Sub-Second Latency
item.description: Cursor

item.title: GitHub Copilot at Scale: How a Hundred Million Suggestions Per Day Actually Works
item.description: Copilot

item.title: How Spotify Uses AI: Embeddings, DJ, and the Recommendation Stack
item.description: From music2vec embeddings to AI-generated narration. How Spotify

item.title: Attention Is All You Need → How Transformers Became the Backbone of Production AI
item.description: The 2017 paper that killed RNNs. What the original Transformer proposed, what the ML world actually shipped from it, and the engineering gaps between

item.title: InstructGPT → How RLHF Turned Raw LLMs Into Products People Actually Use
item.description: OpenAI

item.title: The RAG Paper → From Facebook AI Research to Production Retrieval Systems
item.description: Lewis et al. 2020 introduced RAG as a sequence-to-sequence model. What the original paper proposed, what the industry actually built on top of it, and how modern production RAG is fundamentally different from the original formulation.

item.title: LoRA → How a 2021 Paper Made Fine-Tuning Affordable for Everyone
item.description: Hu et al. introduced Low-Rank Adaptation as a way to fine-tune giant models by training a tiny fraction of parameters. What LoRA does mathematically, why it works, and how QLoRA, AdaLoRA, and LoRA+ evolved it for production use.

item.title: Constitutional AI → How Anthropic
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: How to Answer
item.description: A complete framework for tackling RAG system design questions: how to scope requirements, walk through the architecture, discuss failure modes, and show depth on retrieval quality vs. latency tradeoffs.

item.title: How to Explain Attention Mechanisms in an AI Interview
item.description: What interviewers are testing when they ask about attention, what level of math to show, and how to give a coherent explanation that goes from intuition → math → why it matters in production — without losing your interviewer.

item.title: How to Answer
item.description: Evaluation is where most candidates go blank. This post gives you a reusable framework: task decomposition, metric selection, offline vs. online eval, human labelling, and how to talk about hallucination measurement.

item.title: How to Answer
item.description: ReAct, tool use, memory, multi-agent orchestration — what a senior interviewer wants to hear, what separates a junior answer from a strong one, and a worked example of walking through an agent architecture under time pressure.

item.title: How to Answer
item.description: Prompt caching, model routing, KV cache, quantisation, batching, context compression — a systematic framework for attacking LLM cost. Structured to walk through in an interview in under 10 minutes.

item.title: What Changed: Base LLMs vs. Reasoning Models
item.description: o1, o3, Claude 3.7 Sonnet — what makes a

item.title: Thinking Budget: How to Control Reasoning Model Quality and Cost
item.description: Reasoning models let you dial how much they think. What token budgets mean, the quality-vs-cost frontier, when to use 1K vs 32K thinking tokens, and how to build a dynamic routing system that picks the right thinking depth per query.

item.title: When to Use Reasoning Models (and When Not To)
item.description: Reasoning models aren

item.title: The Economics of Reasoning Models: When Is o1 Worth 20x the Price?
item.description: Reasoning models can cost 20x more than GPT-4o. When is that justified? A breakdown of task types, accuracy lift data, cost-per-correct-answer analysis, and a routing architecture that uses cheap models first and escalates to reasoning only when needed.

item.title: Production Patterns for Reasoning Models: Routing, Caching, and Fallback
item.description: How to deploy reasoning models at scale without blowing your budget. Confidence-based routing, result caching for deterministic queries, structured output extraction from long scratchpads, streaming UX for slow TTFT, and graceful fallback strategies.

item.title: Model Context Protocol: What It Is and Why It Matters
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: Build Your First MCP Server in 30 Minutes
item.description: Step-by-step walkthrough of building a working MCP server using the Python SDK. Define tools and resources, expose them over stdio transport, register with Claude Desktop, and call them from Claude. Every line of code explained.

item.title: Claude Deep Dive: Architecture, Training Philosophy, and When to Use It
item.description: Anthropic

item.title: GPT-4o Deep Dive: Native Multimodality, o1 Reasoning, and the OpenAI Model Stack
item.description: How GPT-4o achieves native audio/vision/text processing in one model, what changed from GPT-4 Turbo, the o1/o3 reasoning model branch, and how to choose across the OpenAI model family.

item.title: Gemini Deep Dive: 1M Context, Native Multimodality, and Google
item.description: Gemini

item.title: Grok Deep Dive: xAI
item.description: xAI

item.title: Llama Deep Dive: Meta
item.description: Llama 3

item.title: MCP vs. Function Calling: Which Should You Use?
item.description: MCP and function calling both let LLMs use tools — but they

item.title: DPO vs RLHF vs GRPO: Which Alignment Method Should You Use?
item.description: Three dominant approaches to aligning fine-tuned models with human preferences. How DPO eliminates the reward model, why GRPO is driving reasoning model breakthroughs, and a decision tree for choosing the right method based on your data, compute, and stability requirements.

item.title: Quantization Deep Dive: GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF, and When Each Wins
item.description: Cutting model size 4–8x without destroying quality. How GPTQ calibrates weight rounding with data, why AWQ outperforms on activation-aware pruning, what GGUF enables for CPU inference, and a practical guide to choosing your quantization stack for production.

item.title: AI Governance in Production: Frameworks, Audits, and What Actually Works
item.description: EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and internal governance programs at Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft. What risk tiers mean for your product, how to structure model cards and audit trails, red-teaming cadences, and the governance gaps most teams miss before going to market.

item.title: Multimodal RAG in Production: Images, Tables, and PDFs at Scale
item.description: RAG for documents with embedded images, charts, and tables. ColPali

item.title: Production Fine-Tuning Case Study: From Zero to Deployed Domain Expert
item.description: End-to-end walkthrough of fine-tuning a 7B model for a legal document extraction task. Dataset curation decisions, SFT vs DPO sequencing, evaluation harness setup, LoRA rank selection, quantization for serving, and what broke in production that didn

item.title: Andrej Karpathy: Building Intuition Through Code
item.description: Karpathy

item.title: Simon Willison: The Pragmatist
item.description: Willison writes daily about what actually works with LLMs in production. His prompt injection research changed how engineers think about trust boundaries.

item.title: swyx: The AI Engineer Thesis
item.description: Shawn Wang coined

item.title: Hamel Husain: Evals Are Everything
item.description: Hamel wrote the definitive guide to LLM evals. His core thesis: if you don

item.title: François Chollet: What LLMs Can
item.description: Chollet built ARC-AGI to measure what LLMs systematically fail at. His

item.title: Yann LeCun: Why World Models Are the Path Forward
item.description: LeCun argues LLMs are fundamentally the wrong architecture for AGI. His JEPA world model proposal and

item.title: World Models: The Architecture Beyond Next-Token Prediction
item.description: Why predicting the next token isn

item.title: Mixture of Experts: Getting 671B Quality at 37B Cost
item.description: How MoE gives you a model with 671B parameters that costs the same to run as a 37B dense model. Sparse activation, expert routing, EPLB load balancing, and the failure modes that bite production teams.

item.title: How I
item.description: The full architecture for a production AI search system — query intent classification, hybrid retrieval, LLM reranking, freshness signals, and why the first version almost always gets the retrieval layer wrong.

item.title: How I
item.description: Building an AI code reviewer that developers actually trust. AST-aware chunking, diff-scoped retrieval, explanation generation, calibrating false positive rate, and the feedback loop that makes it smarter over time.

item.title: How I
item.description: A production customer support AI — intent routing, RAG over product docs, escalation triggers, tone compliance checking, and the guardrail architecture that catches the 3% of cases that can destroy trust.

item.title: A2A Protocol: How Agents Talk to Each Other at Scale
item.description: Google

item.title: Implicit Feedback: The Free Label Generator Hidden in Your Traffic
item.description: Every user interaction is a weak label. Click-throughs, reformulations, copy-paste events, session abandonment — how to extract a high-signal feedback stream from production traffic without asking users to rate anything.

item.title: Reward Modeling from Logs: Turning Traffic into Training Signal
item.description: How to build a reward model from implicit feedback logs using Bradley-Terry pairwise preference, DPO from preference pairs, and the contamination traps that silently corrupt your reward signal.

item.title: The Online Evaluation Loop: Making Your Eval Set Self-Refreshing
item.description: Static eval sets go stale. How to pipeline production failures into your eval set automatically, detect distribution shift before it becomes a user complaint, and run shadow evaluations without user exposure.

item.title: Model Merging: Getting SOTA Quality at Zero Training Cost
item.description: SLERP, TIES, DARE, model soup, and LoRA adapter merging — how the open-source community routinely beats fine-tuned models by merging weights in parameter space. What works, what doesn

item.title: The Fine-Tuning Playbook: LoRA, QLoRA, and When to Use Each
item.description: A practical decision framework for fine-tuning LLMs — from full parameter training to 4-bit QLoRA on consumer GPUs.

item.title: RLHF in Production: What Actually Works
item.description: Reward models, PPO instability, reward hacking, and the lessons learned shipping alignment training at scale.

item.title: DPO vs PPO: The Training Alignment Debate
item.description: Why Direct Preference Optimization is replacing PPO in most teams — and the edge cases where PPO still wins.

item.title: Knowledge Distillation at Scale: Teaching Small Models to Think Big
item.description: How to transfer capabilities from large frontier models to smaller, cheaper, deployable ones — and where it breaks down.

item.title: How I
item.description: STT → LLM → TTS architecture, latency budget breakdown, streaming strategies, and where it actually breaks in production.

item.title: How I
item.description: Parsing PDFs, tables, and images; chunking strategies for structured documents; extraction pipelines with citations.

item.title: How I
item.description: Repository-aware context, diff-aware completion, retrieval over codebases, and the eval harness that actually catches regressions.

item.title: Building Reliable Agents: Loops, Tools, and Failure Modes
item.description: How to structure agent loops, tool calling patterns, retry logic, and the five failure modes that kill agent reliability in production.

item.title: Cost-Aware Prompt Engineering
item.description: How to engineer prompts that perform better and cost less. Token budgets by component, the five optimisation levers (output format, few-shot, CoT, prompt caching, model routing), and the iterative optimisation loop.

item.title: RLHF and DPO: How Models Learn to Do What You Want
item.description: The full alignment pipeline — SFT, reward model training, PPO — and why DPO replaced most of it. Includes the Bradley-Terry model, KL penalty mechanics, reward hacking failure modes, and practical tradeoffs between RLHF and DPO.

item.title: Why the Best Model on the Benchmark Isn
item.description: Goodhart

item.title: Hard Negatives: The Training Trick That Actually Improves Retrieval
item.description: Hard negatives — semantically close but intent-misaligned pairs — are the highest-ROI training signal for retrieval models. Three mining strategies with code.
